finding: The Git hook subprocess can rerun `validate:git-head-evidence` against stale or pre-refresh state after the parent governance flow has already generated fresh git-head evidence, causing a legitimate framework commit to fail on a false old-state verdict.
expectedBehavior: Hook and ATM git wrapper lanes should pass the same fresh git-head evidence context into the subprocess, or deterministically regenerate it inside the child before validation, so a successful parent preflight cannot be invalidated by stale child timing.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced on 2026-06-27 while backfilling meaningful fixes onto `origin/main`: `node atm.mjs hook pre-commit --json` passed after fresh evidence generation, but the later Git hook subprocess and governed commit lane fell back to old `validate:git-head-evidence` behavior and forced `git commit --no-verify` despite no real content failure. Related symptoms overlap the frozen-hook timing family tracked in `ATM-BUG-2026-06-23-018`, but this case is specifically about git-head evidence freshness crossing the hook subprocess boundary. Closed on 2026-06-28 after adding a dedicated regression in `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts` that uses the governed reconcile close-window lane to run an explicit parent `hook pre-commit`, verify the staged close packet plus `.atm/history/evidence/git-head.jsonl`, then complete a real child `git commit` through the installed hook path without reintroducing a stale evidence verdict. Validation: `node --strip-types scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts --mode validate`.
followUp: Keep the parent/child freshness regression in `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts`; if this regresses again, treat it as a release-critical git-head evidence handoff failure rather than a docs-only backlog discrepancy.
